pr.title: Move parts of data and other sections into associated .s files, match some of GObj, label and split some DVD code
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large organization-and-matching PR that removed most content from the monolithic asm/data.s and redistributed data/text into associated assembly files, split Dolphin DVD code into dvd.s and dvdfs.s alongside dvdlow.s, labeled a set of DVD functions/variables, and matched several sysdolphin baselib GObj flag-manipulation functions in C. It also fixed MSL ctype symbol labeling by swapping toupper/tolower labels and updating strtoul call annotations.
what_changed: Deleted about 31k lines from asm/data.s, indicating data was moved out of the global catch-all data file into subsystem-associated assembly files.

Added large associated asm chunks for several Melee systems, including asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s, asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s, asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s, asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.s, and smaller additions in fighter, ftbosslib, ftcrazyhand, gmcamera, gmtitle, lbfile, lbvector, and other lb files.
Split Dolphin DVD assembly by adding asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s and asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.s to obj_files.mk and moving/labelling code out of dvdlow.s.

Labeled several DVD functions in include/functions.h, including DVDLowRequestAudioStatus, DVDLowBreak, DVDLowClearCallback, __DVDLowSetWAType, __DVDFSInit, DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, DVDFastOpen, DVDReadAsyncPrio, DVDInit, stateBusy, and DVDCancel.

Labeled DVD-related variables in include/variables.h, including StopAtNextInt, LastResetEnd, ResetOccurred, Breaking, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory, executing, and AutoFinishing.

Converted several GObj proc flag functions from asm/NON_MATCHING stubs into C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.c and removed corresponding assembly for func_80390CAC and func_80390CD4 from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.s.
Changed HSD_GObjProc in gobj.h from a single u8 flags field to bitfields flags_1:1, flags_2:1, and flags_3:2 at offset 0xD, supporting the matched C flag writers.
Corrected MSL ctype labels so the first routine is toupper and the second is tolower, and updated strtoul branch-call annotations from tolower to toupper.
Added a note in tev.c that HSD_RenderInitAllocData is not matching because externs and should match once split properly.
Changed calcprogress.py output wording from completed event count wording: 'completed {} of 51 Event Matches'.
smart_moves: Kept data close to the code that references it by migrating bulk entries out of asm/data.s into associated subsystem .s files, improving searchability and making later C conversion less dependent on a monolithic data blob.
Split DVD code into dvdlow.s, dvd.s, and dvdfs.s and registered the new objects in obj_files.mk, which mirrors library/module boundaries and creates clearer matching units.

Used known SDK-style names for DVD functions and globals instead of leaving address-based func_/lbl_ placeholders, making cross-references such as calls to DVDConvertPathToEntrynum readable in unrelated files like synth.s.

Matched GObj flag routines by modeling the packed flag byte as C bitfields and using small inline helper loops over HSD_GObjProc child chains, replacing hand assembly with concise source while preserving byte-level behavior.
Fixed misleading symbol labels in MSL ctype/strtoul without changing instruction bytes, improving semantic correctness of call labels for future library matching.
decomp_lessons: When an asm/data.s catch-all grows too large, moving constants, strings, and tables into the owning module’s .s file is valuable even before those modules are fully decompiled.
Build files must be updated when splitting assembly objects; here dvd.s and dvdfs.s were added to TEXT_O_FILES so the split still links in the expected order.

SDK/library code benefits strongly from canonical names: once functions like DVDInit, DVDFastOpen, DVDReadAsyncPrio, and DVDConvertPathToEntrynum are identified, other subsystems can update call annotations to meaningful names.
Packed flags can be decompiled by first refining the struct layout. The HSD_GObjProc flags byte became explicit bitfields, enabling C assignments to match rlwimi-style bit insertion code.
Inline helpers are a useful tactic for repeated tiny loops that mutate linked-list fields; they reduce duplicate C while still giving the compiler an opportunity to emit matching code.
Comment-only or label-only fixes matter: mislabeled toupper/tolower symbols in MSL code would mislead later analysis even if the underlying assembly was already correct.
Extern placement and data splitting affect matchability; the tev.c note explicitly states one non-matching function should match after externs are split properly.
naming_conventions: Dolphin DVD functions were renamed from func_-address placeholders to SDK-like names such as DVDInit, DVDFastOpen, DVDReadAsyncPrio, DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, DVDCancel, DVDLowBreak, DVDLowClearCallback, and __DVDFSInit.

Dolphin DVD globals were renamed from lbl_804Dxxxx placeholders to descriptive state names such as StopAtNextInt, LastResetEnd, ResetOccurred, Breaking, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory, executing, and AutoFinishing.
Internal helper-style GObj routines retained address-based names func_80390C5C, func_80390C84, func_80390CAC, and func_80390CD4; no evidence in this slice shows semantic names for them.
New C helper names in gobj.c use the subsystem prefix and purpose pattern GObj_SetFlag1_inline and GObj_SetFlag2_inline.
Bitfield names in HSD_GObjProc were introduced as flags_1, flags_2, and flags_3, preserving uncertainty while distinguishing the packed bits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved assembly/data section contents into subsystem-specific files while preserving labels and alignment, reducing central data.s churn and improving ownership mapping.

Split Dolphin DVD assembly into separate objects matching functional areas: low-level DVD operations in dvdlow.s, higher-level DVD operations in dvd.s, and filesystem initialization/path handling in dvdfs.s.
Replaced assembly for small GObj flag setters with C loops over proc->child, using bitfield assignments to reproduce prior rlwimi writes to byte offset 0xD.
Used inline C helpers for repeated flag propagation patterns so the generated code could match multiple wrapper functions.
Performed label correction in MSL ctype by swapping toupper and tolower symbol names, then updated strtoul call annotations to call toupper at the affected sites.
Preserved evidence of non-matching blockers with an explicit tev.c comment about externs and future split work.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace address-based GObj names func_80390C5C, func_80390C84, func_80390CAC, or func_80390CD4 with semantic names.
Search for subsequent PRs that decompile Dolphin DVD C files after this dvd.s/dvdfs.s split.
Search for later work that moves the data introduced into asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s or asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s into C source.
Search for later fixes to HSD_GObjProc flag bitfield naming or layout.
Search for PRs resolving the tev.c note about HSD_RenderInitAllocData externs and matching after proper split.
